The company said that it produced a total of 451,758 vehicles between April and June of this year, including 442,936 Model 3 and Model Y vehicles, as well as 8,822 […]
content: Tesla just released its second-quarter delivery and production report, showing that the automaker is starting to recover after a particularly brutal sales year in 2025. The company said that it produced a total of 451,758 vehicles between April and June of this year, including 442,936 Model 3 and Model Y vehicles, as well as 8,822 “other vehicles” like the Cybertruck and the Tesla Semi. (The company discontinued the Model S and X earlier this year.) That represents about a 10 percent increase compared to the second quarter of 2025, when the company produced 410,244 vehicles. Tesla also said that it delivered a total of 480,126 vehicles, including 467,762 Model 3 and Model Y vehicles, as well as 12,364 other vehicles — about a 25 percent increase compared to the second quarter of 2025, when it delivered 384,122 vehicles. (For a direct-to-consumer company like Tesla, deliveries are a proxy for sales.) Last quarter, Tesla said its sales were up 6 percent year over year. Wall Street investors were expecting Tesla to report sales of 400,000-420,000 vehicles for the second quarter. The report comes as Tesla is battling a string of negative headlines surrounding the safety of its partially automated driving technology. Earlier this month, a woman was killed in her home after a Tesla driver using Full Self-Driving crashed into it. Tesla blamed the driver, but the crash prompted the National Transportation Safety Board to open an investigation. Several days later, it was reported that Tesla quietly settled a lawsuit stemming from another fatal crash involving FSD. Meanwhile, Tesla’s robotaxi service is operating at a much slower and smaller scale than Elon Musk originally predicted. The service relies on a fleet of approximately 60 to 70 Model Y vehicles operating in a geofenced area in Austin, Houston, and Dallas.
chineseTitle: 特斯拉第二季度销量增长 25%
chineseSummary: 特斯拉刚刚发布了第二季度交付和生产报告，显示这家汽车制造商在经历了 2025 年销售尤为惨淡的一年后开始复苏。该公司表示，今年 4 月至 6 月期间共生产了 451,758 辆汽车，其中包括 442,936 辆 Model 3 和 Model Y 车辆，以及 8,822 辆 […]
chineseContent: 特斯拉刚刚发布了第二季度交付和生产报告，显示该汽车制造商在经历了 2025 年销售尤为惨淡的一年后开始复苏。该公司表示，今年 4 月至 6 月期间共生产了 451,758 辆汽车，其中包括 442,936 辆 Model 3 和 Model Y 车辆，以及 8,822 辆 Cyber​​truck 和 Tesla Semi 等“其他车辆”。 （该公司今年早些时候停产了 Model S 和 X。）这与 2025 年第二季度（该公司生产了 410,244 辆汽车）相比增加了约 10%。特斯拉还表示，其共交付了 480,126 辆汽车，其中包括 467,762 辆 Model 3 和 Model Y 车辆，以及 12,364 辆其他车辆——与 2025 年第二季度的交付量 384,122 辆相比增长约 25%。 （对于特斯拉这样的直接面向消费者的公司来说，交付量是销量的代表。）特斯拉表示，上季度其销量同比增长 6%。华尔街投资者预计特斯拉第二季度销量将达到 40 万至 42 万辆。该报告发布之际，特斯拉正面临一系列围绕其部分自动驾驶技术安全性的负面新闻。本月早些时候，一名妇女在家中被一名使用全自动驾驶技术的特斯拉司机撞死。特斯拉将责任归咎于司机，但事故促使国家运输安全委员会展开调查。几天后，有报道称，特斯拉就另一起涉及 FSD 的致命事故引发的诉讼悄然达成和解。与此同时，特斯拉的机器人出租车服务的运行速度比埃隆·马斯克最初预测的要慢得多，规模也小得多。该服务依赖于在地理围栏内运行的约 60 至 70 辆 Model Y 车辆组成的车队
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
